description:
§41.15.8 (a) compile-time rejection — schemaFor(User) is called inside a markup body (an ordinary

interpolation) WITHOUT an enclosing block. The function-call form is canonical INSIDE blocks only (its output is a table-declaration fragment that requires the parser context), so a call in any other markup context is rejected with E-SCHEMAFOR-INVALID-CALL-CONTEXT.
rationale: §41.15.8 normative statement: schemaFor(...) calls outside a <schema> block (at the top level of a logic context, inside a <program> body without an enclosing <schema>, or inside any other markup context) SHALL emit E-SCHEMAFOR-INVALID-CALL-CONTEXT (§34; per OQ-SCH-1 + OQ-SCH-2 verdicts). Resolution: wrap the call inside a <schema>${ schemaFor(...) }</> block.
